2604.16037.title: Stochasticity in Tokenisation Improves Robustness
2604.16037.paper_summary: The widespread adoption of large language models (LLMs) has increased concerns about their robustness. Vulnerabilities in perturbations of tokenisation of the input indicate that models trained with a deterministic canonical tokenisation can be brittle to adversarial attacks. Recent studies suggest that stochastic tokenisation can deliver internal representations that are less sensitive to perturbations. In this paper, we analyse how stochastic tokenisations affect robustness to adversarial attacks and random perturbations. We systematically study this over a range of learning regimes (pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, and in-context learning), data sets, and model architectures. We show that pre-training and fine-tuning with uniformly sampled stochastic tokenisations improve robustness to random and adversarial perturbations. Evaluating on uniformly sampled non-canonical tokenisations reduces the accuracy of a canonically trained Llama-1b model by 29.8%. We find that training with stochastic tokenisation preserves accuracy without increasing inference cost.
2604.16037.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的廣泛採用增加了對其穩健性的擔憂。<lb/>輸入的標記化擾動中的脆弱性表明，使用確定性標準標記化訓練的模型可能對對抗性攻擊非常脆弱。<lb/>最近的研究表明，隨機標記化可以提供對擾動不那麼敏感的內部表示。<lb/>在本文中，我們分析隨機標記化如何影響對對抗性攻擊和隨機擾動的穩健性。<lb/>我們系統地研究這一點，涵蓋一系列學習模式（預訓練、監督微調和上下文學習）、數據集和模型架構。<lb/>我們顯示，使用均勻抽樣的隨機標記化進行預訓練和微調可以提高對隨機和對抗性擾動的穩健性。<lb/>在均勻抽樣的非標準標記化上進行評估，會使得標準訓練的Llama-1b模型的準確率降低29.8%。<lb/>我們發現，使用隨機標記化進行訓練可以在不增加推理成本的情況下保持準確性。
2604.16037.author: Sophie Steger et.al.
2604.16037.authors: Sophie Steger, Rui Li, Sofiane Ennadir, Anya Sims, Arno Solin, Franz Pernkopf, Martin Trapp
